sections.title: Getting started
Talking to Grish

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there, what's going on here?

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: Hey yous creature...wha's you's doing here? Yous be cleverer to be running so da sickies from da zogres don't dead ya.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm just looking around thanks.

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: Yous creature won'ts see muchly in dis place...just da zogries coming wiv da sickies.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean sickies?

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: Da zogries comin wiv da sickies...yous get bashed by da zogries and get da sickies...den you gonna be like da zogries.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, I just don't understand...

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: Da sickies is when yous creature goes like orange till green and then goes 'Urggghhhh!' ~ Grish imitates falling down with only the white of his eyes visible. ~

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are Zogres?

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: Da Zogres are da bigun nasties wiv da sickies, deys old pals of Grish but deys jig in Jiggig when dey's full home is deep in da dirt, dey's is not da same dead'uns like was before.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I help in any way?

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: Sorry, yous creatures, but yous is too green behind da ears for dis job Grish finks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'm not!

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: Yes you are!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'm not!

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: Yes you are and that's final!

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: Yes creatures...yous does good fings for Grish and learn why Zogries at Jiggig and den get da Zogries back in da ground.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, so you want me to find out why the Zogres have appeared and then find a way of burying them?

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: Is what Grish says! But dis is da biggy danger fing yous creatures...yous be geddin' sickies most surely...yous needs be ready..wiv da foodies un da glug-glugs.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, so you think there's a good chance that I can get ill from this, so I need to get some food and something to drink?

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: Yea creatures, yous just say what Grish says...not know own wordies creature?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm, sorry, it sounds a bit too dangerous.

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: Yous creature is not a stoopid one...stays out of dere, like clever Grish. Yous can paint circles on chest and be da Shaman too!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm, is it too late to reconsider?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, I'll check things out then and report back.

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: Is yous creatures really, really sure yous wanna do dis creatures..we's got no glug-glugs for da sickies? We's knows nuffin for da going of da sickies?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I'm really sure!

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: Dats da good fing yous creature...yous does Grish a good fing. But yous know dat yous get sickies and mebe get dead!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If that's your idea of a pep talk, I have to say that it leaves a lot to be desired.

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: Yous creatures is alus says funny stuff...speaks proper like Grish!

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: Der's yous go creatures...da best me's do for yous...and be back wivout da sickies.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, I have to go.

sections.title: Talking to Grish after declining to start the quest

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: Hey yous creature's come back den?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I want to help you out and find out about the zogres.

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: Yous creatures does da good fings for Grish and learn why Zogries at Jiggig and den get da Zogries back in da ground.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, thanks.

sections.title: Talking to Grish after starting the quest

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: Yous creature dun da fing yet? Da zogries going in da dirt full home?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nope, I haven't figured out why the zogres are here yet.

sections.title: Talking to the Ogre guard

lines.speaker: Ogre guard
lines.text: Yous needs ta stay away from dis place...yous get da sickies and mebe yous goes to dead if yous da unlucky fing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But Grish has asked me to look into this place and find out why all the undead ogres are here.

lines.speaker: Ogre guard
lines.text: Ok, dat is da big, big scary, danger fing! You's sure you's wants to go in?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I'm sure.

lines.speaker: Ogre guard
lines.text: Ok, I opens da stoppa's for yous creature.

lines.speaker: Ogre guard
lines.text: Ok der' yous goes!

sections.title: Entering Jiggig
Climb-down Stairs
Climb-up Stairs
Entering the north-west room
Search Broken Lecturn
Search skeleton
Talking to the zombie

lines.speaker: Zombie
lines.text: Raaarrrggghhh

sections.title: Open ruined backpack
Search Ogre Coffin
Use knife on Ogre Coffin
Search Ogre Coffin after unlocking it

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Urrrgggg.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Urrrgggg.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aarrrgghhh!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aarrrgghhh!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Raarrrggggg! Yes!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Raarrrggggg! Yes!

sections.title: Search the Ogre Coffin after it is opened
Read Torn page
Look-at Dragon inn tankard
Look-at Black prism
Using the Dragon inn tankard on Grish

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found this tankard in the tomb, have you got any suggestions?

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: Das a good drinker for da drinkies dat un is...is a small-un for Grish so yous creature keeps it yes. Yous creature keeps da fimble drinkers for da smaller drinkies.

sections.title: Using the Torn page on Grish

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This torn page was on a lectern in the tomb, do you know why?

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: Dat's der wizzy stuff, not Ogery stuffsies like what Grish got. Das not even big enough for empty da big blower on! No use for Grish dat creatures...you's keeps it.

sections.title: Using the Black prism on Grish

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey Grish, I found this in the tomb, do you know what it is?

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: Whas you's shuvvin wizzy stuff in Grish face...is a pretty one but dat's more stuff for da wizzy's dan Grish.

sections.title: Investigating B.Vahn
Using the Dragon inn tankard on the Dragon Inn Bartender
Using the Dragon inn tankard on the Dragon Inn Bartender again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again. Can you tell me what you know about this tankard again please?

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: Oh yes, Brentle's tankard. Yeah, you've shown me this already. It belonged to Brentle Vahn, he was quite a common customer, though I've not seen him in a while.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there, I found this tankard in an ogre tomb cavern. It has the emblem of this Inn on it and I wondered if you knew anything about it?

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: Oh yes, this is Brentle's mug...I'm surprised he left it just lying around down some cave. He's quite protective of it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Brentle you say? So you knew him then?

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: Yeah, this belongs to 'Brentle Vahn', he's quite a common customer, though I've not seen him in a while.

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: He was talking to some shifty looking wizard the other day. I don't know his name, but I'd recognise him if I saw him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm, I'm sorry to tell you this, but Brentle Vahn is dead - I believe he was murdered.

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: Noooo! I'm shocked...

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: ...but not surprised. He was a good customer...but I knew he would sell his sword arm and do many a dark deed if paid enough.

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: If you need help bringing the culprit to justice, you let me know.

sections.title: Using the torn page on the Dragon Inn Bartender

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any clue what this might be?

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: Oooh, don't show me that sort of stuff, it's probably all magical and wizardy, probably turn me into a frog as soon as I look at it...

sections.title: Using the black prism on the Dragon Inn Bartender

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there, I found this black prism, I wondered if you knew anything about it.

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: Hmmm, it's not really familiar to me, sorry I don't know what it's for, looks magical to me...maybe someone else in Yanille can help you?

sections.title: Talking to Sithik Ints before talking to Zavistic Rarve

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: Hey...who gave you permission to come in here! Get out, get out I say.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, alright...keep your night cap on.

sections.title: Use Black prism on Sithik Ints

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, what's this then, can you explain it?!

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: Err..it looks sort of familiar, did you steal it from me? Come to think of it, you have the appearance of a common thief!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found it in a place called Jiggig where some undead ogres happen to be wandering around.

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: Oh, nothing to do with me then, never seen it in my life before!

sections.title: Use Torn page on Sithik Ints

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you ever seen anything like this before?

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: It's probably a piece of rubbish someone threw away...what does it say, I can't read it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You should be able to read it, it's been torn from a book on necromancy and you're meant to be a specialist in the subject.

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: Oh, no..., not really a specialist, just a hobby of mine really. Hardly know anything about it, but it does seem interesting...

sections.title: Use Dragon inn tankard on Sithik Ints

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What about this then? Guess where I found this?

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: You probably found it at the local brewhouse! It doesn't take a genius to figure that one out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aha! But I found this in an old ogre tomb! I suspect it's a clue which will lead me to the suspect.

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: Hmmm, well that eliminates all the local people who don't actually drink at the 'Dragon Inn'. When do you think you'll start questioning the remaining population of Yanille?

sections.title: Search Drawers, Cupboard, or Wardrobe

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: Hey! What do you think you're doing?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Erk! I'd better not start rifling through peoples things without permission.

sections.title: Taking Cup of tea by Sithik Ints

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: Hey! What do you think you're doing? Leave my tea alone!

sections.title: Ringing the Bell outside the Wizards' Guild

lines.speaker: Zavistic Rarve
lines.text: What are you doing ringing that bell?! Don't you think some of us have work to do?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I was told to ring the bell if I wanted some attention.

lines.speaker: Zavistic Rarve
lines.text: Well...anyway...we're very busy here, hurry up what do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There's some undead ogre activity over at 'Jiggig', and the ogres have asked me to look into it. I think I've found a clue and I wonder if you could take a look at it for me?

lines.speaker: Zavistic Rarve
lines.text: Hmmm, well this is an uncommon spell component. On its own it's useless, but with certain necromantic spells it can be very powerful. Did you find anything else there?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I found this...

lines.speaker: Zavistic Rarve
lines.text: Hmmm, no, that's not really associated with this to be honest. Did you find anything else there?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not really.

lines.speaker: Zavistic Rarve
lines.text: I don't know what to say then, there isn't enough to go on with the clues you've shown me so far. I'd suggest going back to search a bit more, but you may just be wasting your time?

lines.speaker: Zavistic Rarve
lines.text: Hmm, but this prism does seem to have some magical protection. Once you've finished with this item, bring it back to me would you? I may have a reward for you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sure...I mean, I'll try if I remember.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There's some undead ogre activity over at Jiggig, I've found a clue that you may be able to help with.

lines.speaker: Zavistic Rarve
lines.text: Hmm, this is a half torn spell page, it requires another spell component to be effective. Did you find anything else there?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I found this...

lines.speaker: Zavistic Rarve
lines.text: Hmmm, no, that's not really associated with this to be honest. Did you find anything else there?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not really.

lines.speaker: Zavistic Rarve
lines.text: I don't know what to say then, there isn't enough to go on with the clues you've shown me so far. I'd suggest going back to search a bit more, but you may just be wasting your time?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There's some undead ogre activity over at Jiggig, I've found some clues, I wondered if you'd have a look at them.

lines.speaker: Zavistic Rarve
lines.text: Hmmm, now this is interesting! Where did you get these from?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I got them from a nearby Ogre tomb, it's recently been infested with zombie ogres and I'm trying to work out what happened there.

lines.speaker: Zavistic Rarve
lines.text: This is very troubling [player name], very troubling indeed. While it's permitted for learned members of our order to research the 'dark arts', it's absolutely forbidden to make use of such magic.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any leads on people that I might talk to regarding this?

lines.speaker: Zavistic Rarve
lines.text: Well a wizard by the name of 'Sithik Ints' was doing some research in this area. He may know something about it. He's lodged at that guest house to the North, though he's ill and isn't able to leave his room.

lines.speaker: Zavistic Rarve
lines.text: Why not go and talk to him, poke around a bit and see if anything comes up. Let me know how you get on. However, I doubt that 'Sithik' had anything to do with it.

lines.speaker: Zavistic Rarve
lines.text: Hmm, that black prism seems to have some magical protection. Once you've finished with this item, bring it back to me would you. I may have a reward for you.

sections.title: Talking to Zavistic Rarve inside the guild

lines.speaker: Zavistic Rarve
lines.text: What are you doing...Oh, it's you...sorry...didn't realise...what can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Zavistic Rarve
lines.text: What are you doing bothering me? Don't you think some of us have work to do?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I thought you were here to help?

lines.speaker: Zavistic Rarve
lines.text: Well... I am, I suppose, anyway... we're very busy here, hurry up, what do you want?

sections.title: Use the black prism on Zavistic Rarve after he has already it

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found this black prism at Jiggig where the undead ogre activity was happening?

lines.speaker: Zavistic Rarve
lines.text: Yes, you've already showed me that, bring it to me when you've resolved the problems at Jiggig and I'll see what I can do.

sections.title: Use the torn page on Zavistic Rarve

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I've found a clue from the Jiggig area where the undead ogre activity is happening.

lines.speaker: Zavistic Rarve
lines.text: Hmm, this is a half torn spell page, it requires another spell component to be effective. Did you find anything else there?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not really.

lines.speaker: Zavistic Rarve
lines.text: I don't know what to say then really, there isn't enough to go on here as it is. I'd suggest going back to search a bit more, but you may just be wasting your time.

sections.title: Talking to Sithik Ints

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: Hey...who gave you permission to come in here!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Zavistic Rarve said that I could come and talk to you and ask you a few questions.

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: Oh, Zavistic...why...why would he send you to me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know anything about the undead ogres at Jiggig?

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: Er...undead ogres...no, sorry, no idea what you're talking about there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm, is that right...

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: Well, yes, yes it is. If I knew something, I'd tell you.

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: Anyway, dead ogres you say? How strange? That must be a strange sight?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Very well, if you don't know anything about it, you won't mind if I look around then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you do?

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: I'm a scholarly student of the magical arts. When I was younger I used to be an adventurer, probably just like yourself. But I lost interest in the constant fighting, looting and gaining abilities.

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: Instead I decided to focus my attention and time to study the purer form of the lost arts.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The lost arts? What are they?

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: Ignorant people call them the 'dark arts'. I'm talking about Necromancy, the power to bring the dead back to life - the power of the gods! Surely the most awesome power known to man.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm, well I guess I must be an ignorant person then, because bringing the dead back to life sounds very unnatural.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you mind if I look around?

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: Well, err....well, actually yes I do mind...it's my place and I don't want strangers going through my things.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I'm going to have a look around anyway, if you're not involved in this whole thing, you won't have anything to hide.

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: Why, if I was a few years younger I'd give you a good hiding!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sure!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you mind if I look around?

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: I've already told you that I do! But you'll probably just ignore me again!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Quite right!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, thanks.

sections.title: Search Wardrobe
Read Book of 'h.a.m'

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmmm, Sithik must really hate monsters then, I wonder if he hates ogres in particular?

sections.title: Using the book of 'h.a.m' on Sithik Ints

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's this then?

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: What do you mean? It's a book by the respected HAM leader Johanhus Ulsbrecht, that man speaks for a lot of people who are unhappy with the current state of affairs.

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: Can you honestly tell me that you've not had to fight for your life against the odd monster or two?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm, that may be true, but I don't universally hate all monsters, whereas I have a sneaking suspicion that you do...and ogres in particular!

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: Hmm, that's an interesting theory, care to back it up with any facts?

sections.title: Using the book of 'h.a.m' on Johanhus Ulsbrecht

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, I've found this, do you know anything about it?

lines.speaker: Johanhus Ulsbrecht
lines.text: Yes, in fact I do. I wrote many essays for this book, it was a great seller, many people are stirred by the words and feelings in such a tome.

sections.title: Search Cupboard
Read Necromancy book
Use Necromancy book on Sithik Ints

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aha! A necromantic book! What's this doing here then?

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: Oh..I'm not quite sure actually...where did you find that then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found it in this cupboard! What do you have to say for yourself?

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: Oh yes, that's right...I remember now. It's for my research, there's nothing really dangerous about it, unless it falls into the wrong hands. I'm sure it's pretty safe with me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmmm, likely story!

sections.title: Using the torn page on Irwin Feaselbaum

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found this torn page, do you know anything about it?

lines.speaker: Irwin Feaselbaum
lines.text: Hey...I hope you didn't rip that page out of one of those books, they'll be trouble if it was.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, it wasn't actually, I got it from somewhere else, do you recognise it?

lines.speaker: Irwin Feaselbaum
lines.text: Hmm, well it certainly looks like text from one of the books. Have you checked at the wizards' guild in Yanille? We sometime loan books to them.

sections.title: Using the necromancy book on Irwin Feaselbaum

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi, do you know anything about this book?

lines.speaker: Irwin Feaselbaum
lines.text: Hey...that book has been loaned to the wizards' guild in Yanille, I hope you're not returning it in a damaged condition!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I was just wondering where the book came from actually and try to get some more information about it.

lines.speaker: Irwin Feaselbaum
lines.text: Well, that book looks as if it's been through the wars...you're not going to return it in that condition. You take it back to the wizards at Yanille, they borrowed it, so it's their responsibility.

sections.title: Search Drawers
Read the Book of portraiture
Use Book of portraiture on Sithik Ints

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, so explain this then?

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: It's my hobby...I'm interested in portraiture, but all art in general. It's fun, you should try it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I do it...

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: Well...you could start by reading the book!

sections.title: Use papyrus on Sithik Ints

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: Oh lovely! You're making my portrait! Let me see it afterwards!

sections.title: Using the bad portrait on Sithik Ints

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here you go, what do you think?

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: Hmmm, well it's an interesting interpretation, but not really classic realist representation is it? It's not my favourite, but I like the 'truth' of the work...well done.

sections.title: Using the good portrait on Sithik Ints

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here you go, what do you think?

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: Hmmm, well it's not the most flattering of portraits, but I like the 'honesty' of the work...well done.

sections.title: Using the bad portrait on Zavistic Rarve

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look, I made a portrait of Sithik.

lines.speaker: Zavistic Rarve
lines.text: Who the demonikin is that? Is it meant to be a portrait of Sithik, it doesn't look anything like him!

sections.title: Using the good portrait on Zavistic Rarve

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look, I made a portrait of Sithik.

lines.speaker: Zavistic Rarve
lines.text: Hmm, great...but I already know what he looks like!

sections.title: Using the bad portrait on the Dragon Inn Bartender

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: Who's that? I mean, I guess it's a picture of a person isn't it? Sorry...you've got me? And before you ask, you're not putting it up on my wall!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's a portrait of Sithik Ints...don't you recognise him?

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: I'm sorry, I really am, but I just don't see it...can you make a better picture?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll try...

sections.title: Using the good portrait on the Dragon Inn Bartender

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: Yeah, that's the guy who was talking to Brentle Vahn the other day! Look at those eyes, never a more shifty looking pair will you ever see!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm, you've just identified the man who I think sent Brentle Vahn to his death.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm trying to bring him to justice with the Wizards' Guild grand secretary. Do you think you could sign this portrait to say that he was talking to Brentle Vahn.

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: I can and I will!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Many thanks for your help, it's really very good of you.

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: Not at all, just doing my part.

sections.title: Look-at Signed portrait
Using the Signed portrait on Sithik Ints

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, what do you think of this? I'm going to show it to Zavistic and you're going to be in trouble!

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: Hmmm, well, I've got quite a common looking face, I'm often mistaken for other wizards, you know, when I'm wearing my wizard's hat, robes and staff. There's a lot of us around here you know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think so! This is a signed picture of you, someone recognised you, you're in deep trouble!

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: Ok, I'll pay you to keep this secret - how much do you want for the picture?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You can't buy me Sithik!

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: Ok, let's say two million...two million to keep quiet and give me the picture.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh...erm...well, that is a lot of money actually...er....

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: Yes, and you deserve it, you're very clever! Now, take the money...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I won't take the money, I'm going to bring you to justice!

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: Oh well, suit yourself! I wasn't going to give you the money anyway! No one will believe some crazy adventurer and an Inn keep.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, I'll shut up for two million!

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: Ha! Ha! You believed me! I'm not going to give you all my money! No one will believe a crazy adventurer and a local Inn keep!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're a mean and cruel man Sithik, a mean and cruel man!

sections.title: Talking to Zavistic Rarve after gathering evidence

lines.speaker: Zavistic Rarve
lines.text: What are you doing...Oh, it's you...sorry...didn't realise...what can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What did you say I should do?

lines.speaker: Zavistic Rarve
lines.text: You should go and have a chat with Sithik Ints, he's in that house just to the north. He's a lodger and has a room upstairs. Just tell him that I sent you to see him. He should be fine once you've mentioned my name.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where is Sithik?

lines.speaker: Zavistic Rarve
lines.text: He's in that house just to the north, less than a few seconds walk away. He's a lodger and has a room upstairs...he's not very well though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have an item that I'd like you to look at.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have some items that I'd like you to look at.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have this necromancy book as evidence that Sithik is involved with the undead ogres at Jiggig.

lines.speaker: Zavistic Rarve
lines.text: Yeah, you've shown me this before...if this is all the evidence you have?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Please just look at it again...

lines.speaker: Zavistic Rarve
lines.text: Ok, let me look then.

lines.speaker: Zavistic Rarve
lines.text: Ok, so he's researching necromancy...it doesn't mean anything in itself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, but if you look, you can see that there is a half torn page which matches the page I found at Jiggig.

lines.speaker: Zavistic Rarve
lines.text: Hmm, yes, but someone could have stolen that from him and then gone and cast it without his permission or to try and deliberately implicate him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look, this book proves that Sithik hates all monsters and most likely Ogres with a passion.

lines.speaker: Zavistic Rarve
lines.text: Yeah, you've shown me this before...if this is all the evidence you have?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Please just look at it again...

lines.speaker: Zavistic Rarve
lines.text: Ok, let me look then.

lines.speaker: Zavistic Rarve
lines.text: So what, hating monsters isn't a crime in itself...although I suppose that it does give a motive if Sithik was involved. On its own, it's not enough evidence though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This is the tankard I found on the remains of Brentle Vahn!

lines.speaker: Zavistic Rarve
lines.text: Yeah, you've shown me this before...if this is all the evidence you have?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Please just look at it again...

lines.speaker: Zavistic Rarve
lines.text: Ok, let me look then.

lines.speaker: Zavistic Rarve
lines.text: That doesn't mean anything in itself, you could have gotten that from anywhere. Even from the Dragon Inn tavern! There isn't anything to link Brentle Vahn with Sithik Ints.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This is a portrait of Sithik, signed by the landlord of the Dragon Inn saying that he saw Sithik and Brentle Vahn together.

lines.speaker: Zavistic Rarve
lines.text: Yeah, you've shown me this before...if this is all the evidence you have?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Please just look at it again...

lines.speaker: Zavistic Rarve
lines.text: Ok, let me look then.

lines.speaker: Zavistic Rarve
lines.text: Hmmm, well that is interesting.

lines.speaker: Zavistic Rarve
lines.text: However, there isn't enough evidence for me to take the issue further at this point. If you find any further evidence bring it to me.

lines.speaker: Zavistic Rarve
lines.text: And I'm starting to think that Sithik may be involved. Here, take this potion and give some to Sithik. It'll bring on a change which should solicit some answers - tell him the effects won't revert until he's told the truth.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, I have to go.

sections.title: Talking to Zavistic Rarve after getting the potion

lines.speaker: Zavistic Rarve
lines.text: What are you doing...Oh, it's you...sorry...didn't realise...what can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Zavistic Rarve
lines.text: Have you used that potion yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, actually, I've lost it, could I have another one please?

lines.speaker: Zavistic Rarve
lines.text: Sure, but don't lose it this time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, not yet, what was I supposed to do again?

lines.speaker: Zavistic Rarve
lines.text: Try to use the potion on Sithik somehow, he should undergo an interesting transformation, though you'll probably want to leave the house in case there are any side effects. Then go back and question Sithik and tell

lines.speaker: Zavistic Rarve
lines.text: him the effects won't wear off until he tells the truth. In fact, that's not exactly true, but I'm sure it'll be an extra incentive to get him to be honest.

sections.title: Talking to Sithik Ints after convincing Zavistic Rarve

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: What do you want now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey there's no need to be rude!

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: What do you expect when you just go snooping around a person's place against their express permission.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you do?

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: I'm a scholarly student of the magical arts. When I was younger I used to be an adventurer, probably just like yourself. But I lost interest in the constant fighting, looting and gaining abilities.

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: Instead I decided to focus my attention and time to study the purer form of the lost arts.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The lost arts? What are they?

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: Ignorant people call them the 'dark arts'. I'm talking about Necromancy, the power to bring the dead back to life - the power of the gods! Surely the most awesome power known to man.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm, well I guess I must be an ignorant person then, because bringing the dead back to life sounds very unnatural.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why do you spend most of your time in bed?

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: I'm actually quite old and not so very well and I'd like to get over this illness I have, then I'll return to my very serious and important studies.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, thanks.

sections.title: Searching the Drawers, wardrobe, or cupboard after convincing Zavistic Rarve
Using strange potion on Sithik Ints

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here, try some of this potion, it'll make you feel better!

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: Err, yuck....no way am I taking any potions or medication off you...I don't trust you!

sections.title: Using strange potion on cup of tea
Talking to Sithik Ints in ogre form

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: Arghhhh..what's happened to me...you beast!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's your own fault, you shouldn't have lied about your involvement with the undead Ogres at Jiggig. The potion will wear off once you've told the truth!

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: Ok, ok, I admit it, I got Brentle Vahn to cast the spell to put an end to those awful Ogres...they're just disgusting creatures...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, that's a start...now I want some answers.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I remove the effects of the spell from the area? The ogres want to get their ceremonial dance area back and can't do that with undead walking all over it.

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: Unfortunately you can't. The spell is permanent, it will last forever, the only option you have is to move the ceremonial area.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're an evil man and I'm going to make you pay for this...you can stay like that forever as far as I'm concerned.

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: No...no, let me try to make amends...please I can help you. Just don't leave me like this.

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: Haven't I told you this already? You can't remove the spell, it's permanent, it will last forever, the only option you have is to move the ceremonial area.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're an evil man and I'm going to make you pay for this...you can stay like that forever as far as I'm concerned.

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: No...no, let me try to make amends...please I can help you. Just don't leave me like this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I get rid of the undead ogres?

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: Ok, similar spells have been cast before and the only way to deal with the resulting creatures is to cordon off the area and not go in there again.

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: The undead creatures usually manifest some sort of disease so it's best to attack them from a distance with a ranged weapon.

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: Normal missiles like arrows and darts do very little damage to them because they're designed to destroy internal organs. This is a waste of time with undead creatures like undead ogres.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, clearly so what should we use?

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: From my research it looks like a flat ended arrow was designed called a 'Brutal arrow'. This does large amounts of crushing damage to the creature. You can make them by using larger arrows.

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: I think some Ogre hunters make them. But instead of adding an arrow tip, you hammer a large nail into the end of the shaft.

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: Haven't I already explained this to you once before?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Humour me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I get rid of the disease?

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: My research shows that two jungle based herbs can be used, one is found near river tributaries and looks like a vine, the other is found in caves and grows on the wall.

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: It's quite well camouflaged so it's unlikely that you'll find it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We'll see about that!

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: Haven't I already explained this disease thing to you once before?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just tell me again or else I'll never turn you back into a human!

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: No...noo...please, I'll tell you.

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: Haven't I already explained this disease thing to you once before?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just tell me again or I'll turn you back into an ogre.

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: No...noo...please, I'll tell you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, I have to go.

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: But...you can't just leave me here like this!

sections.title: Talking to Ogre Sithik again

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: Arghhhh..what do you want now...you've turned me into a beast!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've got some questions for you...and you'd better answer them well or else!

lines.speaker: Sithik Ints
lines.text: Ok, ok, I'll tell you anything, just turn me back into a human again!

sections.title: Talking to Zavistic Rarve after giving Sithik the potion

lines.speaker: Zavistic Rarve
lines.text: What are you doing...Oh, it's you...sorry...didn't realise...what can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Zavistic Rarve
lines.text: Don't you worry about Sithik, he's not likely to be moving from his bed for a long time. When he eventually does get better, he's going to be sent before a disciplinary tribunal, then we'll sort out what's what.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for your help with all of this.

lines.speaker: Zavistic Rarve
lines.text: Ooohh, no thanks required. It's I who should be thanking you my friend...your investigative mind has shown how vigilant we really should be for this type of evil use of the magical arts.

sections.title: Defeating the Zogres
Talking to Uglug Nar (before using Relicym's balm on him)

lines.speaker: Uglug Nar
lines.text: Me's not got no glug-glugs to sell, yous bring me da sickies glug-glug den me's open da stufsies for ya.

sections.title: Use Relicym's balm on Uglug Nar

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, here you go! I brought you some of the potion which should cure the disease. You said that you would buy some from me.

lines.speaker: Uglug Nar
lines.text: Yous creatures done da good fing...yous get many bright pretties for dis...!

sections.title: Talking to Grish
Talking to Grish after losing the Ogre gate key

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: Yous creature got da old fings yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've lost the key you gave me!

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: Yous stupid creatures....luckily Grish has 'nother one..

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: Yous creatures doesn't loosing this ones.

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: Yous creature dun da fing yet? Da zogries going in da ground?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found who's responsible for the Zogres being here.

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: Where is da creature? Me's wants to squeeze him till he's a deadun...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The person responsible is a wizard named 'Sithik Ints' and he's going to be in serious trouble. He told me that the spell which raised the zogres from the ground will last forever.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry to say, but you'll have to move the site of your ceremonial dancing somewhere else.

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: Dat is da bad fing creature...we's needs new Jiggig for da fallin' down jig.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, that's right, you'll need to create a new ceremonial dance area.

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: Urghhh...not good fing creature, yous gotta get da ogrish old fings for da making new jiggig special. You's creature needs da key for getting in da low bury place.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, so you want me to go back in there and look for something for you?

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: Yeah creature, yous gotta get da ogrish old fings for da making new jiggig and proper in da special way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sithik told me how to make Brutal arrows which means I can kill these zogres from a distance!

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: Uhggh, whas you's sayin' creature? Yous speakies too stupid for Grish...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know how to make large arrows...you know, 'big stabbers', to kill the zogres...they're bigger and apparently do a lot of damage, only thing is, the normal ogre bow I need to fire it is quite slow.

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: Why you's not say so creature...me's shows you how to make da bigger stabber chucker... ~ Grish gets a couple of items out of his back pack.~

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: De're creature...now yous is makin' da bigga stabber chucker...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks! I think....

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I also found out that the disease can be cured.

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: Dat's da good fing creature, yous do good fing to give un to Uglug...he gives bright pretties for da sickies glug glug..

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have some other questions for you.

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: Oh yes creatures...what's other fings yous wanna know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why are you much nicer than the Shaman in Gu'Tanoth?

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: Dey's is da big crazy one's! Dey's biggest angries wiv fings and wanna dead all fings...dey's gotten da biggies wizzy stuff...and dey's wanna eat yous creatures...Grish, not do dat...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, well that's a relief! It's good to know you don't eat humans...

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: Grish not say dat! Me's want's tasty looking creatures for yums...you's looks like da sickies chompy...not good for da gutsies...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Gulp!

lines.speaker: Uglug Nar
lines.text: Grish, you's is fright da creatures! Leave it alone!

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: But it's da big laffsies when it's facey goes to whiteness....ha ha ha!

lines.speaker: Uglug Nar
lines.text: But it's not da big yumsies when it's gone to all frighty...

lines.speaker: Uglug Nar
lines.text: ha ha ha ha!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah...very funny, I'm sure.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know Rantz?

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: Me's know's about Rantz, he's da biggun chompy hunter..he finks...ha ha ha!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do you mean?

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: He's da bad shot chompy sticker, no good at sneaky, sneaky part, he's more gooder at da 'noisy, noisy miss da chompy', ha ha ha!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why doesn't Rantz live with the rest of the Ogres?

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: He's been leaving Gu 'Noth 'cos dey's peoples is da big stressy dere? All da ogries is busying all da time...not doin' no good for da healfy fing. Rantz is da brave-un tho! He's got da big secret fing for leaving Gu' Noth

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: but me's not knowin it. But maybe's he's just want's to be da better chompy sticker?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why do you call this place Jiggig?

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: It's da place where da Jiggig is done...we's jig at Jiggig...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I want to talk about the quest

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, I have to go.

sections.title: Talking to Grish after getting the Ogre gate key

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: Yous creature got da old fings yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nope, not yet.

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: Yous gets 'em quick tho, cos we'ze wonna do da new Jiggig place...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There must be an easier way to kill these zogres!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sithik told me how to make Brutal arrows which means I can kill these zogres from a distance!

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: Uhggh, whas you's sayin' creature? Yous speakies too stupid for Grish...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know how to make large arrows...you know, 'big stabbers', to kill the zogres...they're bigger and apparently do a lot of damage, only thing is, the normal ogre bow I need to fire it is quite slow.

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: Why you's not say so creature...me's shows you how to make da bigger stabber chucker... ~ Grish gets a couple of items out of his back pack.~

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: De're creature...now yous is makin' da bigga stabber chucker...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks! I think....

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There must be a way to cure this disease!

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: Did yous creature makes da sickies glug glug and putin some wiv Uglug for bright pretties? He's goodun for makin' da glug glugs...yous maken da glug-glug, den sellin' one for Uglug, he's makin' more of da sickies glug

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: glug and sellin' for bright pretties to yous creature...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, I have to go.

sections.title: Open Ogre stone door
Search Stand
Search the stand again
If you flee from Slash Bash or if he despawns
Talking to Grish after killing Slash Bash

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: Hey, you's creature got da old fings?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No sorry, I don't have them yet.

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: Yous creatures get dem for me soon doh, yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, I have them here!

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: Dat is da goodly fing yous creature, now's we's can make da new Jiggig place away from zogries! Yous been da big helpy fing yous creature, Grish wishin' yous good stuff for da next fings for creature.

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: ~ Grish seems very pleased about the return of the artefacts. ~

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, that's very nice of you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How's everything going now?

lines.speaker: Grish
lines.text: All da zogries stayin' in da oldie Jiggig, we's gonna do da new Jiggig someways else. Yous creature da good- un for geddin' da oldie fings...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, I have to go.
